title: hieracium brevifolium subsp. caruelii
intro: Hieracium brevifolium subsp. caruelii, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its yellow flowers and serrated leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems. The medicinal properties of Hawkweed can also be attributed to its ability to stimulate circulation, reduce fever, and act as an antibacterial agent. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations.
